intro.title: Around the Equator
intro.body: Amelia Earhart and Fred Noonan attempted to circumnavigate the world along the equator in a Lockheed Electra 10E in 1937. They left Miami eastbound on 1 June and disappeared between Lae and Howland Island on 2 July. Earhart sent dispatches to the Herald Tribune at each major stop.

stops.name: Miami
stops.note: Departure 1 June 1937 at 5:56 am. The Electra heads south-east across the Caribbean.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia plate. Florida airfield at dawn, twin-engine silver monoplane with NR16020 on the rudder, two aviators in leather jackets at the cockpit door.

stops.name: Natal
stops.note: Brazilian Atlantic port. Last stop before the open-ocean crossing to West Africa, 7 June 1937.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia plate. Tropical airfield with palms, mechanics under the wing, low-wing monoplane being refuelled by hand.

stops.name: Karachi
stops.note: Arrival 17 June 1937 after legs through West Africa and Khartoum. Earhart phones George Putnam in California from Karachi.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia plate. Imperial Airways flying-boat hangar at Karachi, Electra on the apron beside it, ground crew in shorts and pith helmets.

stops.name: Lae (New Guinea)
stops.note: Last confirmed position. Take-off 0000 GMT 2 July 1937 with 1,100 US gallons of fuel for the 2,556-mile leg to Howland.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia plate. Grass airstrip on a jungle bay, Electra taking off down the slope toward the sea, cargo ship offshore.

stops.name: Howland Island (target)
stops.note: Never reached. The Itasca's radio operator hears Earhart at 8:43 GMT — 'We are running on north and south' — then nothing. The search continues today.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia plate. Tiny coral island under empty Pacific sky, US Coast Guard cutter Itasca below, smoke trail signal rising — no aircraft visible.
